title: AI Chief of Staff Skill-Orchestrated Agent Architecture
summary: Implementation started for the `AI Chief of Staff` composition root, the Chief of Staff skill registry/contract, and the first skill-backed builder path that unifies travel logistics, follow-through, and briefing under one assistant identity.
next_action: Ship the first vertical slice: the new `ai_chief_of_staff` behavior, skill adapters for the existing domain modules, and builder support for launching the unified assistant.

verification.notes: Implementation is in progress. The first target slice is the new behavior shell, skill registry, adapter-based orchestration of current travel/follow-through/briefing modules, and builder wiring for `AI Chief of Staff`.

resume_context.last_completed_step: Created the initial architecture spec and moved the work into implementation.
pending_steps: Add the new `ai_chief_of_staff` behavior and skill registry
Wrap the existing follow-through, travel, and briefing modules as internal skills
Wire the unified assistant into the builder and validation surfaces
Run project verification and review the implementation against the spec
open_questions: Whether Slack follow-through should stay bundled inside the initial `followthrough` skill or become its own skill later
Whether assistant-level prioritization should take over Telegram staging directly in v1 or remain additive metadata first
resume_context.notes: The implementation plan intentionally starts with adapter-based reuse of the existing deterministic travel and follow-through systems. Shared Gmail/Calendar/Slack acquisition remains a later phase after the top-level assistant shell is working.

history.note: Initial draft created for a skill-oriented `AI Chief of Staff` architecture that consolidates travel, follow-through, and briefing under one user-facing assistant.

Implementation began for the new `ai_chief_of_staff` behavior, the Chief of Staff skill registry/contract, adapter-based orchestration of the current travel and follow-through modules, and builder support for launching the unified assistant.
